title: auth — outbound webhooks
description: Auth DISPATCHES a small, security-relevant set of outbound webhook events for tenants that opt in. Payloads are HEAVILY redacted — identity fingerprints, no email, no dob, no IPs. Distinct from identity substrate (which dispatches ZERO webhooks); auth's events are legitimately useful for tenant-side integrations (session-management dashboards, SIEM feeds).

outbound_webhook_kinds.notes: Sanitised for SIEM ingest — no email, no dob, no IP. Consumers can correlate via identity_fingerprint across events.

outbound_webhook_kinds.trigger_event: PasswordResetCompleted OR identity::PasswordChanged (auth listens + re-dispatches for tenants that need the notification-adjacent version)

payload.note: This is a P0 security signal — tenant SIEM should trigger investigation.

outbound_webhook_kinds.notes: Impersonation start is legally required disclosure to the target User. This webhook is a nice-to-have secondary channel for tenants whose SIEM pipeline includes impersonation alerts.

inbound_webhook_kinds.name: kms.key.rotated
inbound_webhook_kinds.description: Optional AWS EventBridge integration — KMS notifies when the key backing JWT-signing-key encryption rotates. Received event triggers RotateJwtSigningKeyJob for the affected Application(s).
inbound_webhook_kinds.notes: Best-effort. Missing the event is not catastrophic — the monthly cron rotation cadence also fires.

rationale: Auth-lifecycle webhooks are appropriate at this level — the payload contains ONLY fingerprints + coarse event metadata (guard, application_id, reason). No PII, no credentials, no session tokens.

Identity substrate deliberately publishes ZERO outbound webhooks (see modules/identity/blueprints/identity/webhooks.json) because its events carry too much PII to sanitise cleanly. Auth's events are already redacted-to-fingerprint at the event-payload layer (see events.json), so surfacing them via webhook is safe.
